title: ABC谋杀案
summary:
Hercule Poirot and Captain Hastings discuss an anonymous letter that Poirot finds unsettling. While Hastings suggests the letter is merely a poorly executed prank by a drunkard, Poirot expresses deep unease, noting that his experience tells him something is wrong with it. Poirot explains to Hastings that he would find a robbery on the 21st comforting, as it would mean the letter's threat was not a murder. Meanwhile, Alexander Bonaparte Caster, a tall man with a hunchbacked and squinting appearance, sits in a shabby bedroom on Thursday, June 20th, marking a name on a printed list.

On the 21st, Inspector Japp of Scotland Yard visits Poirot and Hastings. Japp reports that the events predicted for the 21st were merely minor disturbances: a broken store window and two people being drunk and disorderly. This news relieves Poirot, who admits he may have been mistaken in taking the letter seriously. Japp dismisses such letters as common hoaxes written by people seeking a thrill. After Japp leaves, Poirot and Hastings engage in lighthearted banter regarding Hastings' thinning hair. Poirot eventually reflects on his own heightened sensitivity, comparing his concern over the letter to a blind watchdog barking at nothing. He concludes by stating that if he were to choose a type of crime to investigate, he would choose murder.
